mapping.a_element: stochastic (noise) forcing of the metastable energy-balance model
mapping.b_element: demographic noise in the finite-population Hawk-Dove game

mapping.a_element: reduced transfer-operator / slow-mode projection separating fast bulk relaxation from the slow escape direction
mapping.b_element: timescale separation used to reduce the game dynamics

brief_justification: The overlap is only generic: both are noise-driven metastable stochastic systems analyzed with a fast/slow (timescale) reduction. But A's actual transferable machinery -- Ruelle--Pollicott resonance spectral-gap closing, extreme-value tail statistics, and DAHM phase decoherence used as early-warning diagnostics of an approaching tipping -- has no counterpart in B, whose engine is demographic-noise-induced emergence of polymorphism in an evolutionary game; the specific governing processes do not correspond role-for-role.
